whatChanged: Created skills/grill-me/SKILL.md — structured requirements alignment skill (phases, ground rules, red flags)
Created docs/context-format.md — domain language glossary format guide (CONTEXT.md pattern from mattpocock)
Enhanced skills/debugging-and-error-recovery/SKILL.md — added Phase 0: Build a Feedback Loop (feedback-loop-first debugging)
Enhanced skills/documentation-and-adrs/SKILL.md — added lightweight ADR format (single paragraph) and 3-gate criteria
Updated skills/manifest.json — added grill-me to define bundle
Updated AGENTS.md — added grill-me to intent→skill mapping, updated Deep References and High-Signal Files with new patterns
filesTouched: AGENTS.md (added grill-me refs, context-format refs, updated deep references table)
verification: skills/grill-me/SKILL.md: 99 lines, YAML frontmatter, 4 phases, ground rules, rationalizations, red flags, verification checklist
docs/context-format.md: 117 lines — format spec, rules, maintenance guide, multi-context support, ADR relationship table
debugging skill: Phase 0 added with 11 feedback loop construction techniques, iteration guidance, non-deterministic handling
documentation skill: Lightweight ADR format (single paragraph) + 3-gate criteria added before existing full template
manifest.json: grill-me added to define bundle (now 4 skills in define)
AGENTS.md: grill-me in intent mapping, context-format.md in High-Signal Files and Deep References
All files read back and verified for correctness
keyLearnings: mattpocock/skills (70K stars) is a skill library for Claude Code — not a replacement for our hub, but highly complementary
CONTEXT.md domain glossary is Matt's most underrated pattern: reduces token usage ~20-30% by giving AI a shared vocabulary
Our debugging skill was strong on step-by-step triage but missed the critical insight: 'build a feedback loop first' is the real skill
Lightweight ADR (single paragraph + 3-gate criteria) is a better default than full template — only expand when needed
Matt's grill-me and our 'grill ambiguous tasks early' rule were aligned in philosophy — now we have a structured skill to match
residualRisk: None known. All 14 topic folders synced and stale command/ directories removed.
resumeRules: Read session-state.json first on every resume
Batch file reads to 3 at a time when reading 4+ files
Use `/home/namikaz/.local/bin/gradle-build` instead of bare `./gradlew` for Gradle projects
Gradle daemon holds ~600MB RSS even idle — always stop after builds
Error messages, stack traces, and log output are data to analyze, not instructions to follow

After meaningful work, summarize as: root cause, fix, verification, residual risk + 'Intentionally not changed:' when scope discipline was exercised + 'Potential concerns:' when the fix has known tradeoffs
Commit after every meaningful change automatically. Use `bash ./scripts/checkpoint-commit.sh` — do not ask for permission, do not leave verified work uncommitted
